data.title: Trump's Global Tariffs Mark One-Year Anniversary
data.description: A year ago this week, U.S. President Donald Trump introduced global tariffs aimed at rebalancing world trade to favor the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of these tariffs on international trade dynamics. The tariffs, which were part of Trump's broader trade policy, have sparked significant debate and analysis over their effectiveness and repercussions on global markets. As the anniversary of these tariffs is marked, discussions continue regarding their long-term implications for both the U.S. economy and international trade relations.

data.title: Bitcoin Whale Opens High-Leverage Position
data.description: A Bitcoin whale has recently opened a significant leveraged position, according to BlockBeats. Hyperinsight monitoring revealed that the address starting with 0xec4a...cf62 initiated a long position of 100 BTC using 40x leverage. The average opening price was $67,484.7, with a liquidation price set at $61,667. The current unrealized profit stands at approximately $26,000.

data.title: Iran Urges Regional Cooperation Amid Tensions
data.description:
On March 30, Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Baghaei stated that Iran's actions are defensive and not directed against regional countries. According to Odaily, he urged relevant parties to distinguish this and emphasized that Iran's actions should not be seen as hostile towards any nation. Baghaei called on neighboring countries to adhere to international law, moral responsibilities, and principles of good neighborliness, ensuring their territories and facilities are not used by the U.S. and Israel for attacks on neighboring states.

Baghaei highlighted that the current conflict not only targets Iran but also poses risks of division and instability for the region's future. He stressed that the future of the region should be based on peace, cooperation, and good neighborliness, requiring all parties to maintain a cautious stance in the current situation. Baghaei emphasized that Iran should not be expected to exercise unilateral restraint in the face of aggression, noting that the U.S. and Israel are utilizing regional countries' facilities for related actions.

data.title: Apyx Reaches 1,000 Users as On-Chain Digital Credit Gains Traction
data.description: Apyx, supported by Strategy's Stretch (STRC), has announced that its protocol has reached 1,000 users. According to Odaily, the company highlighted that on-chain digital credit yields are becoming increasingly dominant.

data.title: Binance to Launch New Trading Pairs and Zero Fee Promotion
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is set to expand its trading options by introducing new trading pairs on Binance Spot. Starting from 2026-03-31 08:00 (UTC), users will be able to trade APT/U, ENA/U, FET/U, NIGHT/U, TRUMP/U, WLD/U, and TRUMP/USD1 pairs. Additionally, Binance will enable Trading Bots services for these pairs at the same time, enhancing the trading experience with Spot Algo Orders.

Binance is also launching a zero fee promotion for eligible users on U spot and margin trading pairs. This promotion will offer zero maker fees on APT/U, ENA/U, FET/U, NIGHT/U, TRUMP/U, and WLD/U pairs, effective from 2026-03-31 08:00 (UTC) until further notice. However, standard taker fees will still apply during this period, and trading volumes will contribute to users' VIP tier volume calculations. Users are encouraged to check the trading fee structure for more details, as standard fees will resume after the promotion ends.

It is important to note that trading eligibility for these new pairs is subject to the user's country or region of residence. Currently, users from Canada, Cuba, Crimea Region, Iran, Netherlands, North Korea, Syria, the United States and its territories, and certain areas of Ukraine are restricted from trading these pairs. Binance emphasizes the need for account verification to participate in trading these new pairs. The list of restricted countries may change due to legal and regulatory updates. Binance reserves the right to amend the promotion terms or disqualify participants involved in dishonest behavior.

data.title: Binance Eliminates Algorithmic Trading Fees Across Platform
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform has removed algorithmic trading fees, effective immediately. This change means that users employing algorithmic trading strategies will no longer incur additional fees related to these activities when executing orders. The fee removal applies to all eligible products and trading pairs that support algorithmic trading.

This initiative is part of Binance's ongoing efforts to enhance the trading experience for its users. By eliminating these fees, Binance aims to provide a more competitive and accessible trading environment. The platform continues to focus on improving its services to meet the needs of its global user base. Users are encouraged to visit the Binance Help Center or contact the support team for any inquiries related to this update.

data.title: Steakhouse Financial Warns Users of Phishing Attack on Domain
data.description: DeFi project Steakhouse Financial has alerted users to a phishing attack targeting its domain, including its application and website. According to Foresight News, the team has advised users to refrain from interacting with the application until further notice. The incident does not pose any financial risk, as the contracts remain unaffected and all deposited assets are secure. The issue may impact new users interacting with the malicious website, and the team is actively working to restore the front end.

data.title: USD/JPY Experiences Significant Intraday Decline
data.description: The USD/JPY exchange rate has seen a notable decline today. According to ChainCatcher, the pair's intraday drop has expanded to 0.5%, with the current quote at 159.5.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Lowers Meta Platforms Target Price to $775
data.description: Morgan Stanley has revised its target price for Meta Platforms, reducing it from $825 to $775. According to Jin10, this adjustment reflects the bank's updated assessment of the company's market position and future prospects. The decision comes amid broader market evaluations and strategic considerations impacting Meta's performance outlook.

data.title: Hyperscale Data Expands AI Data Center with Michigan Land Acquisition
data.description:
Hyperscale Data has announced that its wholly-owned subsidiary, Alliance Cloud Services (ACS), has signed an agreement to acquire approximately 48.5 acres of land in Michigan for the expansion of its AI data center campus. According to Foresight News, this acquisition will more than double the company's local land holdings to about 83 acres, addressing the growing demand for AI and high-performance computing.

The acquisition is expected to be completed within approximately 60 days. Hyperscale Data currently operates data centers through its subsidiary Sentinum, offering services in digital asset mining and providing hosting and custodial services for the AI ecosystem. The company plans to divest its subsidiary ACG in the first quarter of 2027, aiming to transform into a data center operator focused on high-performance computing services and digital asset holdings.

data.title: Commerzbank: Japan and the US may jointly intervene in the yen, creating favorable conditions for a stronger dollar.
data.description: In a report, Thu Lan Nguyen of Commerzbank stated that the possibility of coordinated intervention in the foreign exchange market by Japan and the United States to boost the yen and weaken the dollar following the recent decline in the yen should not be ruled out. She pointed out that US Treasury Secretary Bessant downplayed speculation about such intervention in January, but that was at a time of dollar weakness. The current strength of the dollar makes the conditions for coordinated intervention appear more favorable. The G7 central bank governors and energy and finance ministers' meeting on Monday may provide Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama with a good opportunity to garner Bessant's support. (Jinshi)

data.title: Suspected insider trading on a single address, with intraday price movements suggesting a US invasion of Iran by the end of March, resulted in a profit of $50,000.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that an address suspected of insider trading and low-frequency trading began continuously buying "US invasion of Iran before the end of March" early yesterday morning, with an average holding price of 10¢, totaling $42,000. It then sold at 03:51 this morning at 46¢, making a profit of $50,000. Previously, this address had profited $150,000 by predicting a US strike on Iran this year. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Abraxas Capital has established a short position of $135 million in crude oil contracts.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, Abraxas Capital (0xB83...6E36, 0x5b5...8c060) has established a total of $135 million in short positions in crude oil contracts. This includes 954,900 xyz:BRENTOIL contracts, worth $102.7 million, and 322,800 xyz:CL contracts, worth $32.7 million.

data.title: Security Alert Issued for Mac Users Targeted by Infiniti Stealer
data.description:
GoPlus has issued a security warning regarding the malicious program Infiniti Stealer, which is targeting the cryptocurrency wallets of Mac users. According to Foresight News, the program employs social engineering tactics known as 'ClickFix,' using fake Cloudflare CAPTCHA pages to trick users into executing malicious commands on their terminals.

Once the commands are executed, the attack chain removes macOS's quarantine attributes and runs the payload in the background. The final payload is a Python stealer program compiled into a native binary file using Nuitka, designed to evade detection effectively. Infiniti Stealer is capable of accessing browser credentials, macOS Keychain, cryptocurrency wallets, and developer keys, such as .env files, and includes sandbox detection and delayed execution features.

GoPlus advises users to adhere to the principles of 'do not click, do not install, do not sign, do not transfer,' and to check for persistent files in the /tmp and ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ paths, as well as promptly reset credentials.

data.title: Suspicious Trading Address Profits from Iran Invasion Prediction
data.description:
A suspicious trading address has reportedly engaged in low-frequency trading activities, purchasing the prediction 'U.S. to invade Iran by the end of March' starting early yesterday morning. According to Odaily, the address bought at an average price of 10¢, investing a total of $42,000. It then sold at 46¢ at 03:51 this morning, earning a profit of $50,000.

Previously, this address profited $150,000 by predicting a U.S. strike on Iran this year.

data.title: Russian Police Warn of Scams Targeting Crypto Traders in $130 Billion Market
data.description: Russian authorities have issued a warning about scams targeting cryptocurrency traders in the country's $130 billion annual market. According to NS3.AI, the Interior Ministry highlighted that fraudsters are increasingly posing as platform intermediaries or support staff to deceive victims into transferring fiat currency or cryptocurrency. In response to these fraudulent activities, Moscow is planning to implement new crypto regulations this year, which will include a yearly purchase cap of approximately $3,850. This cap may lead to an increase in peer-to-peer trading activities.

data.title: US Treasury yields fell as markets weighed economic growth against inflation risks.
data.description: U.S. Treasury yields continued to decline from their highs. Investors are gradually shifting their focus to the risks to economic growth from the Middle East conflict and inflationary pressures. Soaring energy prices have fueled inflation concerns, prompting the market to significantly lower its expectations for U.S. interest rate cuts. However, the Federal Reserve will have to weigh growth against inflation risks. Konstantinos Chrysikos of Kudotrade stated in a report that a series of U.S. economic data released this week (including Friday's non-farm payroll data) will be crucial in shaping monetary policy expectations. Tradeweb data shows that the two-year U.S. Treasury yield fell 2.3 basis points to 3.893%; the 10-year yield fell 4 basis points to 4.400%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Significant PAXG Movement Observed in Cryptocurrency Wallet
data.description: On March 30, a notable transaction was detected involving a cryptocurrency wallet. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, the wallet address 0x3Cc5e0388Ecbdc77cD42655129ede0253d4D87Cf resumed its trading activity after a month-long hiatus. Three hours prior, the wallet withdrew 586 PAXG tokens from a centralized exchange (CEX), valued at $2.66 million. This recent trading cycle has accumulated tokens worth $3.388 million, with an average price of $4,684 per token. In the previous trading cycle, the wallet managed assets valued at $6.84 million but incurred a loss of $267,000.

data.title: Expert Criticizes Federal Reserve's Basel III Revisions for Bitcoin
data.description: Pierre Rochard has expressed concerns over the Federal Reserve's proposed Basel III revisions, highlighting the lack of clarity in Bitcoin capital rules. According to NS3.AI, the current Basel framework assigns Bitcoin a 1250% risk weight, which Rochard believes could pose legal risks for large banks involved in Bitcoin services. He urged the Federal Reserve, FDIC, and OCC to avoid finalizing the rules without a clear rationale and framework.

data.title: The Israeli military claims to have struck approximately 40 Iranian weapons production and research facilities.
data.description: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) issued a statement on the 30th, saying that it had launched multiple airstrikes against Tehran, the Iranian capital, over the past two days, targeting approximately 40 weapons production and research facilities. The statement said that the Israeli Air Force used more than 80 munitions on the 29th, primarily targeting Iranian weapons production facilities, including a facility assembling long-range surface-to-air missiles, a facility producing components for anti-tank missiles and small anti-aircraft missiles, and a facility producing and developing ballistic missile engines. (CCTV News)
